name: Merge K sorted Lists
statement: Given heads of k sorted linked lists as an array called heads, merge them into one single sorted linked list and return the head of that list.
constraints:
• 2 <= k <= 100
  • 1 <= number of nodes in each list <= 100
  • -1000 <= values of each node <= 1000
  • All lists are sorted in non-decreasing order.

examples.explanation: head -> -5 -> -4 -> -3 -> -3 -> 1 -> 1 -> 2 -> 2 -> 3 -> 3 -> 4 -> 4 The nodes in bold come from the 3rd list, the underlined nodes come from the 2nd list, rest are from the 1st list.
head -> -5 -> -4 -> -1 -> 10 -> 11 -> 12 The nodes in bold come from the 1st list, rest are from the 2nd list.
